title: How to Print a Message in Java

content.problemStatement:
In Java, printing messages to the console is one of the most basic yet essential programming tasks. The `System.out.print` and `System.out.println()` method is used to display output and is readily available in Java without any additional imports.

**Syntax**
```java
System.out.print("Hello");        //  Prints text but stays on the same line.
System.out.println(" World!");    // Prints text and moves to the next line.
```

---


1. **Printing Text**

To print words, enclose them in double quotes (`""`)
```java
// Using println() for a new line
System.out.println("Hello, World!");    // Output: Hello, World!
// Using print() to stay on the same line
System.out.print("Welcome to Java!");   // Output: Welcome to Java!
```

2. **Printing Numbers**

To print numbers, write them without quotes:
```java
// This prints a whole number
System.out.println(123);      // Output: 123
// This prints a decimal number    
System.out.println(3.14);      // Output: 3.14
```
